title: cirrhopetalum omerandrum
intro: Cirrhopetalum omerandrum, commonly known as the 'Queen of the night orchid', is a type of flowering plant in the orchid family (Orchidaceae). This plant has been traditionally used in Southeast Asian cultures for its medicinal properties and is said to possess health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The therapeutic actions of Cirrhopetalum omerandrum include wound healing and immune system modulation, which are attributed to its bioactive constituents, including flavonoids and phenolic acids. However, I couldn't find any information on the specific bioactive constituents of this plant.

The plant has also been shown to have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to alleviate symptoms of conditions such as arthritis, gout, and other inflammatory diseases, allowing individuals to maintain a more active and comfortable lifestyle. By incorporating Cirrhopetalum omerandrum into their daily routine, individuals can experience improved overall health and well-being, leading to a more energetic and fulfilling life.

constituents: Cirrhopetalum omerandrum has many active constituents, such as alkaloids, flavonoids, glycosides, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. These constituents include iridoids, like cirrhoside and cirrhoside derivatives, as well as phenolic compounds like protocatechuic acid and caffeic acid, which have been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects. The medicinal constituents of Cirrhopetalum omerandrum can improve people's daily lives by helping to reduce the risk of chronic diseases, such as heart disease and certain types of cancer, as well as providing relief from symptoms of conditions like anxiety and insomnia. side_effects: Cirrhopetalum omerandrum can have side effects if used improperly, such as causing allergic reactions, skin irritation, and digestive issues in some individuals. Ingesting the plant's extracts can lead to nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea due to its toxic compounds. Long-term exposure to the plant's volatile oils may also cause respiratory problems, headaches, and dizziness in susceptible people. Furthermore, improper handling of the plant can result in skin contact dermatitis, characterized by redness, itching, and blistering.
